LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMANDA ANDERSON IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS). THE COURT SIGNS A FORMAL ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO,CSR#8600,TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION BY DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL TO STRIKE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.13 (TRANSACTION ID # 62275405) FILED BY DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL A CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMANDA ANDERSON THAT SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE 15657 ALLOWS MS. ANDERSON TO SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES FOR VIOLATION OF THE ELDER ABUSE AND DEPENDENT ADULT CIVIL PROTECTION ACT. AS STATED IN THE ORDER ON THE HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER, MS. ANDERSON HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED A CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF THAT ACT. CCP 425.13 IS NOT APPLICABLE TO A CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF THAT ACT. (COVENANT CARE, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 771, 783.). THE COURT SIGNS A FORMAL ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Marin General Hospital's motion to strike portions of the complaint filed by plaintiff Amanda Anderson that seek punitive damages is denied. Welfare & Institutions Code 15657 allows Ms. Anderson to seek punitive damages for violation of the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act. As stated in the order on the Hospital's demurrer, Ms. Anderson has adequately alleged a claim for violation of that Act. CCP 425.13 is not applicable to a claim for violation of that Act. (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783.). The Court signs a formal order.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT MARIN GENERAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Marin General Hospital's demurrer to the fifth and seventh causes of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and dependent adult abuse in the complaint filed by plaintiff Amanda Anderson is overruled as to both causes of action. For her IIED claim, Ms. Anderson adequately alleges that the Hospital's employees intentionally restrained her and performed a takedown, their actions were intentional, outrageous, malicious and done with the intent to cause harm or with reckless disregard that those actions would cause her to suffer humiliation, mental anguish, and emotional and physical distress, and as a result of the employees' conduct, she suffered emotional and physical distress. For her dependent adult abuse claim in violation of Welfare and Institutions Code 15657, Ms. Anderson pleads, with the required particularity, facts alleging that she was a dependent on the Hospital at the time of the incident, the Hospital's employees physically abused her and battered her by intentionally, improperly, and recklessly restraining her and performing a takedown, she did not consent to the touching, and as a result, she sustained injuries. The Court signs a formal order.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)
